info.title: Devin API v3
info.description: Devin v3 API with Service User authentication and RBAC

schema.title: Response Handle Get Self V3 Self Get

get.description: Get all org-level Devin ACU limits for this enterprise.

put.description: Create or update an org-level Devin ACU limit.

delete.description: Delete an org-level Devin ACU limit.

get.description:
Get daily ACU consumption for the entire enterprise.

Returns total ACUs and consumption broken down by date.

**Timezone behavior**: Billing cycles use midnight PST (Pacific Standard Time)
as the day boundary, which corresponds to 08:00:00 UTC. To match the consumption
data shown in the Devin dashboard, pass Unix timestamps that align with this
timezone offset (e.g., 1733385600 for December 5, 2025 at midnight PST).

Get daily ACU consumption for a specific organization.

Returns total ACUs and consumption broken down by date for the org.

**Timezone behavior**: Billing cycles use midnight PST (Pacific Standard Time)
as the day boundary, which corresponds to 08:00:00 UTC. To match the consumption
data shown in the Devin dashboard, pass Unix timestamps that align with this
timezone offset (e.g., 1733385600 for December 5, 2025 at midnight PST).

Get daily ACU consumption for a specific user.

Returns total ACUs and consumption broken down by date for the user.

**Timezone behavior**: Billing cycles use midnight PST (Pacific Standard Time)
as the day boundary, which corresponds to 08:00:00 UTC. To match the consumption
data shown in the Devin dashboard, pass Unix timestamps that align with this
timezone offset (e.g., 1733385600 for December 5, 2025 at midnight PST).

Get daily ACU consumption for a specific service user.

Returns total ACUs and consumption broken down by date for sessions
created by the given service user.

**Timezone behavior**: Billing cycles use midnight PST (Pacific Standard Time)
as the day boundary, which corresponds to 08:00:00 UTC. To match the consumption
data shown in the Devin dashboard, pass Unix timestamps that align with this
timezone offset (e.g., 1733385600 for December 5, 2025 at midnight PST).

Get daily ACU consumption for a specific session.

Returns total ACUs and consumption broken down by date for the session.

**Timezone behavior**: Billing cycles use midnight PST (Pacific Standard Time)
as the day boundary, which corresponds to 08:00:00 UTC. To match the consumption
data shown in the Devin dashboard, pass Unix timestamps that align with this
timezone offset (e.g., 1733385600 for December 5, 2025 at midnight PST).

parameters.name: session_id
parameters.description: Session ID (prefix: devin-). Alias for devin_id used in consumption endpoints.

get.description:
Get aggregated usage metrics for the enterprise account.

Returns counts of sessions, searches, and PRs (opened, closed, merged)
within the specified time range.

Get aggregated usage metrics for the enterprise account.

Returns counts of sessions, searches, and PRs (opened, closed, merged)
within the specified time range.

Get aggregated session metrics for the enterprise account.

Optionally filter by playbook_id to get metrics for sessions created with a specific playbook.
Get aggregated search metrics for the enterprise account.

Get aggregated PR metrics for the enterprise account.

Optionally filter by playbook_id to get metrics for PRs from sessions created with a specific playbook.

Get daily active users for each day in the specified time range.

A user is considered active on a given day if they have created at least
min_sessions sessions OR at least min_searches searches during that UTC day.

Returns a list of daily active user counts, one entry per day in the range.

Get weekly active users for each week in the specified time range.

A user is considered active in a given week if they have created at least
min_sessions sessions OR at least min_searches searches during that UTC week.

Weeks are defined as Monday 00:00:00 UTC to Sunday 23:59:59 UTC.

Returns a list of weekly active user counts, one entry per week in the range.

Get monthly active users for each month in the specified time range.

A user is considered active in a given month if they have created at least
min_sessions sessions OR at least min_searches searches during that UTC month.

Returns a list of monthly active user counts, one entry per month in the range.
Get session counts and ACU consumption grouped by category and subcategory.

get.summary: Get Active Users for Custom Date Range
get.description:
Get unique active users for a custom date range.

A user is considered active if they have created at least min_sessions sessions
OR at least min_searches searches within the specified time range.

Returns a single count of unique active users across the entire range.

get.description: Get the current organization groups configuration.

put.description:
Update the organization groups configuration.

This endpoint replaces the entire org groups configuration with the provided config.
- Groups not in the request will be deleted
- Groups in the request will be created or updated to match the config

get.description: Return the full folder tree with per-folder note counts.
Get a note by ID.
Get a specific playbook by ID.

post.description:
Trigger a Devin Review for a pull/merge request.

Creates a database record that will be picked up by the review
worker pool. Always fetches the latest commit from the PR.

get.description:
Return the latest Devin Review for a PR scoped to the enterprise.

The owning organization is resolved server-side from ``pr_url`` —
the same way as the trigger endpoint. When ``commit_sha`` is
omitted, the PR's current head commit is fetched from the provider
and used. Returns ``404`` when no review exists for the resolved
commit.

parameters.name: pr_url

schema.description: Full URL of the pull/merge request to look up (e.g. `https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123`).
schema.title: Pr Url

parameters.description: Full URL of the pull/merge request to look up (e.g. `https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123`).

parameters.name: commit_sha

schema.description: Commit SHA to look up (full or short prefix). Defaults to the PR's current head commit when omitted.
schema.title: Commit Sha

parameters.description: Commit SHA to look up (full or short prefix). Defaults to the PR's current head commit when omitted.

get.description:
List sessions with detailed insights including message counts,
session size classification, and AI-generated analysis.
Get details of a specific session.

Get detailed insights for a specific session, including message counts,
session size classification, and AI-generated analysis.

post.description:
Trigger on-demand generation of session insights.

Returns ``already_exists`` if insights have already been generated.
Otherwise kicks off generation in the background. Poll the
GET insights endpoint to retrieve results once generation completes.

get.description: List all sessions across the enterprise.

post.description: Append tags to a session (deduplicating with existing tags).

get.description: List all messages for a session with cursor-based pagination, ordered chronologically.

post.summary: Send a message to a session
post.description: Send a message to an active session. The session will be automatically resumed if suspended.

get.description:
Get the queue status for an enterprise.

Returns the total number of queued sessions (status: new, resuming, claimed)
and a status indicator (normal/elevated/high).

This endpoint is useful for enterprise admins to monitor queue health and
set up alerts for capacity issues.
List users whose organization membership is derived from IDP group assignments.

post.summary: Create API key for service user
post.description:
Create a new API key for a service user.

The caller must have ManageAccountServiceUsers permission.

get.summary: List API keys for service user
get.description: List API keys for a service user, optionally filtered by status.

delete.summary: Revoke API key for service user
delete.description:
Revoke an API key for a service user.

Returns 404 if the key is not found, 409 if already revoked.

post.summary: Rotate API key for service user
post.description:
Rotate an API key for a service user.

Creates a new key. By default revokes the old key; set revoke_current=false
for graceful rollover where both keys remain active temporarily.
Returns 404 if the key is not found, 400 if the key is not active.

post.summary: Create an org- or repo-tier blueprint
post.description:
Create a blueprint.  ``type`` is inferred from ``repo_name``.

Per spec, this never auto-triggers a build — callers must
``POST /builds`` explicitly.

patch.summary: Update a blueprint's contents and/or position
patch.description:
Partial update for a single blueprint. Does NOT auto-trigger a build.

Fields honored:

- ``contents`` — writes a new YAML version for the blueprint.
- ``position`` — sets the repo-tier blueprint's execution position.
  Returns ``400`` on an org-tier blueprint (those have no position).

Returns ``400`` when the body is empty.

get.summary: Presigned URL for the blueprint's YAML

post.description: Manually trigger a snapshot build using the org's current blueprints.

parameters.name: active

schema.description: If true, returns 0 or 1 items: the build whose image is currently used by new sessions.
schema.title: Active

parameters.description: If true, returns 0 or 1 items: the build whose image is currently used by new sessions.

post.summary: Pin the org to a specific successful build
post.description:
Pin a successful build (less than 7 days old).

Translates the SDK's 400 errors into 409 (per spec: "409 if the
build is not succeeded, or older than 7 days").

delete.summary: Unpin a build (no-op if not pinned)
delete.description:
Unpin the org if and only if it is currently pinned to ``build_id``.

No-op (still 204) when the org is not pinned to this build.

get.summary: Presigned URL for the build's log file
List repositories available to an organization

put.description: Idempotently enables indexing for a batch of repositories and triggers indexing jobs.

schema.title: Response Handle Bulk Index Repositories V3Beta1 Organizations  Org Id  Repositories Indexing Put

delete.description: Disables indexing and clears configured branches for a batch of repositories.

schema.title: Response Handle Bulk Remove Repositories From Indexing V3Beta1 Organizations  Org Id  Repositories Indexing Delete

put.description: Idempotently enables indexing for a single repository and triggers indexing jobs.

delete.description: Disables indexing and clears configured branches for a single repository.

get.summary: Get indexing status for a repository

ActiveUserMetricsListResponse.title: ActiveUserMetricsListResponse
ActiveUserMetricsListResponse.description: Validated list of active user metrics with ascending order and no overlapping periods.

ActiveUserMetricsResponse.title: ActiveUserMetricsResponse
ActiveUserMetricsResponse.description: Single entry for active users over time.

ApiKeyResponse.title: ApiKeyResponse
ApiKeyResponse.description: Shared response model for API key details (used by both PAT and service user endpoints).

token.title: Token
token.description: The raw API token. This is only shown once at creation/rotation time.

ApiKeyWithTokenResponse.title: ApiKeyWithTokenResponse
ApiKeyWithTokenResponse.description: Shared response model for API key creation/rotation (includes one-time token).

Blueprint.title: Blueprint
Blueprint.description:
Public ``Blueprint`` resource shape.

Note: the ``type`` enum may grow in the future; clients MUST gracefully
handle unknown values.  YAML contents are fetched separately via
``GET /blueprints/{id}/contents``.

BlueprintList.title: BlueprintList
BlueprintList.description: List envelope for blueprints (returned in execution order).

Build.title: Build
Build.description:
Public ``Build`` resource shape — opaque "did this succeed yet?" view.

Per spec, no platform/snapshot/per-job introspection is exposed.  Future
additions (``triggered_by_repo``, build metadata, etc.) are additive.

expires_at.title: Expires At
expires_at.description: Optional expiration as a UNIX timestamp in seconds. Must be in the future if provided.

contents.title: Contents
contents.description: YAML body for the new blueprint. Omit to create an empty blueprint.

repo_name.title: Repo Name
repo_name.description: If set, creates a repo-tier blueprint for that repository. If omitted, creates an org-wide (org-tier) blueprint.

contents.title: Contents
contents.description: Optional initial YAML body for the blueprint.

DevinBrainUserSelf.title: DevinBrainUserSelf
DevinBrainUserSelf.description: Identity information for an authenticated Devin brain session.

FolderSummary.title: FolderSummary
FolderSummary.description: One folder in the knowledge folder tree.

IdpGroupUser.title: IdpGroupUser
IdpGroupUser.description: A user whose membership is derived from IDP group assignments.

IdpRoleAssignment.title: IdpRoleAssignment
IdpRoleAssignment.description: A role assignment inherited via IDP group membership.

OrgGroupsConfig.title: OrgGroupsConfig
OrgGroupsConfig.description: Configuration mapping group names to their settings.

PatUserSelf.title: PatUserSelf
PatUserSelf.description: Identity information for an authenticated PAT user.

macro.title: Macro
macro.description: Playbook macro identifier. Must start with '!' followed by one or more letters, digits, underscores, or hyphens. Example: '!my_macro' or '!my-macro'

prs_created_count.title: Prs Created Count
prs_created_count.description: Total PRs Devin authored across all states (open + merged + closed).

prs_opened_count.title: Prs Opened Count
prs_opened_count.description: PRs Devin authored that are currently open.

prs_merged_count.title: Prs Merged Count
prs_merged_count.description: PRs Devin authored that were merged.

prs_closed_count.title: Prs Closed Count
prs_closed_count.description: PRs Devin authored that were closed without merging.

prs_taken_over_count.title: Prs Taken Over Count
prs_taken_over_count.description: Total PRs Devin took over across all states (open + merged + closed). A take-over is when Devin pushed commits to a PR it did not originally create.

prs_taken_over_opened_count.title: Prs Taken Over Opened Count
prs_taken_over_opened_count.description: PRs Devin took over that are currently open.

prs_taken_over_merged_count.title: Prs Taken Over Merged Count
prs_taken_over_merged_count.description: PRs Devin took over that were merged.

prs_taken_over_closed_count.title: Prs Taken Over Closed Count
prs_taken_over_closed_count.description: PRs Devin took over that were closed without merging.

pr_url.title: Pr Url
pr_url.description: Full URL of the pull/merge request to review (e.g. `https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123`).

status.title: Status
status.description: Current review status. Newly accepted reviews start as `pending`.

commit_sha.title: Commit Sha
commit_sha.description: Commit SHA of the PR head that will be reviewed.

expires_at.title: Expires At
expires_at.description: Unix timestamp at which the URL expires.

new_key_expires_at.title: New Key Expires At
new_key_expires_at.description: Optional expiration for the new key as a UNIX timestamp in seconds. Null for no expiration.

revoke_current.title: Revoke Current
revoke_current.description: Whether to revoke the current key. Set to False for graceful rollover.

platform.title: Platform
platform.description: VM platform for sessions spawned by this schedule (e.g. 'windows'). When omitted, sessions fall back to the org's default platform at trigger time. Value must match a platform configured for the organization (case-insensitive); unknown values are rejected with a 400 that lists the available platform labels.

run_as_user_id.title: Run As User Id
run_as_user_id.description: Set the user ID that this schedule will run as. Requires ImpersonateOrgSessions permission. Setting to null reverts to the default bot user. Omitting the field leaves the current identity unchanged.

platform.title: Platform
platform.description: VM platform for sessions spawned by this schedule (e.g. 'windows'). When omitted, the platform is left unchanged. Value must match a platform configured for the organization (case-insensitive); unknown values are rejected with a 400 that lists the available platform labels.

ServiceUserSelf.title: ServiceUserSelf
ServiceUserSelf.description: Identity information for an authenticated service user.

SessionAttachment.title: SessionAttachment
SessionAttachment.description: An attachment sent during a session.

SessionCountsByOrigin.title: SessionCountsByOrigin
SessionCountsByOrigin.description:
Session counts by origin type, exposed on the v3 metrics API.

A curated subset of `SessionOrigin`: legacy origins (cli,
vscode_extension, devin_spaces) are intentionally not surfaced.
Counts for sessions with a `scheduled` DB origin roll into
`automation` via the canonical `db_origin_to_api` mapping, since
automations are a superset that schedules will migrate into.

structured_output_schema.title: Structured Output Schema
structured_output_schema.description: JSON Schema (Draft 7) for validating structured output. Max 64KB. Must be self-contained (no external $ref).

structured_output_required.title: Structured Output Required
structured_output_required.description: When true (default), the agent MUST call provide_structured_output with is_final=true before its turn ends. When false, the tool is available but not required — it is not guaranteed to be called in a given turn.

platform.title: Platform
platform.description: Override the VM platform for the session (e.g. 'windows'). When omitted (or set to 'inherit'), a session created by a parent Devin inherits the parent's platform; otherwise the organization default is used. Any value must match a platform configured for your organization (case-insensitive); unrecognized values are rejected with a 400 whose error body lists the available platform labels for the org.

devin_mode.title: Devin Mode
devin_mode.description: Override the Devin agent mode for the session. 'normal' uses the default Agent mode, 'fast' uses Fast mode, and 'lite' uses Devin Lite. Fast and Lite are subject to the same feature flag and enterprise agent preview restrictions as the web app.

SessionInsightsAnalysis.title: SessionInsightsAnalysis
SessionInsightsAnalysis.description: AI-generated analysis data from a completed session analysis.

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: The session ID for which insights generation was triggered.

status.title: Status
status.description: The status of the generation request.

SessionInsightsGenerateResponse.title: SessionInsightsGenerateResponse
SessionInsightsGenerateResponse.description: Response from triggering session insights generation.

category.title: Category
category.description: The session's assigned use-case category, if categorisation has run. Only populated on get/list endpoints.

subcategory.title: Subcategory
subcategory.description: The session's assigned subcategory display name. 'Other' when a category is set but no subcategory was assigned or resolved. Only populated on get/list endpoints.

origin.title: Origin
origin.description: The origin from which the session was created.

structured_output.title: Structured Output
structured_output.description: Validated structured output from the session. Only populated on get/list endpoints.

status_detail.title: Status Detail
status_detail.description: Additional detail about the session's current status. When status is 'running': 'working' (actively working), 'waiting_for_user' (needs user input), 'waiting_for_approval' (awaiting action approval in safe mode), or 'finished' (task complete). When status is 'suspended': the reason for suspension such as 'inactivity', 'user_request', 'usage_limit_exceeded', 'out_of_credits', 'out_of_quota', 'no_quota_allocation', 'payment_declined', 'org_usage_limit_exceeded', 'total_session_limit_exceeded', or 'error'. Only populated on get/list endpoints.

num_user_messages.title: Num User Messages
num_user_messages.description: Number of user messages sent during the session.

num_devin_messages.title: Num Devin Messages
num_devin_messages.description: Number of Devin messages sent during the session.

session_size.title: Session Size
session_size.description: Session size classification based on ACU usage and message count.

analysis.description: AI-generated session analysis. None if analysis has not completed.

SessionInsightsResponse.title: SessionInsightsResponse
SessionInsightsResponse.description:
Session details augmented with quantitative metrics and AI analysis.

Extends SessionResponse with additional fields that require extra queries.

SessionMessage.title: SessionMessage
SessionMessage.description: A message in a Devin session.

category.title: Category
category.description: The session's assigned use-case category, if categorisation has run. Only populated on get/list endpoints.

subcategory.title: Subcategory
subcategory.description: The session's assigned subcategory display name. 'Other' when a category is set but no subcategory was assigned or resolved. Only populated on get/list endpoints.

origin.title: Origin
origin.description: The origin from which the session was created.

structured_output.title: Structured Output
structured_output.description: Validated structured output from the session. Only populated on get/list endpoints.

status_detail.title: Status Detail
status_detail.description: Additional detail about the session's current status. When status is 'running': 'working' (actively working), 'waiting_for_user' (needs user input), 'waiting_for_approval' (awaiting action approval in safe mode), or 'finished' (task complete). When status is 'suspended': the reason for suspension such as 'inactivity', 'user_request', 'usage_limit_exceeded', 'out_of_credits', 'out_of_quota', 'no_quota_allocation', 'payment_declined', 'org_usage_limit_exceeded', 'total_session_limit_exceeded', or 'error'. Only populated on get/list endpoints.

SessionSecretInput.title: SessionSecretInput
SessionSecretInput.description: Input model for a session secret provided via API.

TriggerBuildBody.title: TriggerBuildBody
TriggerBuildBody.description: ``POST /builds`` body. Empty per spec; reserved for future fields.

contents.title: Contents
contents.description: New YAML body for the blueprint.

contents.title: Contents
contents.description: If set, replaces the blueprint's YAML body with a new version.

position.title: Position
position.description: If set, updates the execution position of this repo-tier blueprint. Positions are not required to be unique; ties are broken arbitrarily by the server when sorting for execution.

UpdateOrgBlueprintBody.title: UpdateOrgBlueprintBody
UpdateOrgBlueprintBody.description:
Partial update for an org- or repo-tier blueprint.

Any subset of fields may be provided. Fields not set are left alone.
Providing ``position`` on an org-tier blueprint returns ``400`` — only
repo-tier blueprints have a position. Does NOT trigger a build.

UserWithIdpRoles.title: UserWithIdpRoles
UserWithIdpRoles.description: User with both direct and IDP-group-derived role assignments.

WindsurfSessionUserSelf.title: WindsurfSessionUserSelf
WindsurfSessionUserSelf.description: Identity information for an authenticated Windsurf session user.
